metadata.description: Idiom -> literal-meaning gloss knowledge base consumed by the idiom-aware resolution module (stage 2). Keys are surface idiom forms; each entry maps to a literal English paraphrase used for substitution or as a translation hint.
metadata.license_note: This seed set contains short, common-knowledge English idiom paraphrases authored for this project (source tag 'seed'). To EXPAND coverage at scale, run build_glosses.py, which intersects the MAGPIE idiom inventory (CC-BY-4.0) with a glossed source. VERIFY the license of any external gloss source before redistributing the merged KB; record its license here.

the ball is in your court.literal: it is your turn to act

barking up the wrong tree.literal: pursuing a mistaken course of action

beat around the bush.literal: avoid speaking directly about the topic

a blessing in disguise.literal: something that seems bad but is actually good

hit the nail on the head.literal: describe exactly what is causing a situation

the last straw.literal: the final problem that makes a situation unbearable

a dime a dozen.literal: very common and of little value

speak of the devil.literal: the person we were talking about has appeared

bury the hatchet.literal: end a quarrel and make peace

cry over spilt milk.literal: be upset about something that cannot be undone

the tip of the iceberg.literal: a small visible part of a much larger problem

a hot potato.literal: a controversial issue that is awkward to handle

back to the drawing board.literal: start planning again from the beginning
